name: Git Rebase Linux
description: Bash snippet that defines and runs a helper to stash local work when needed, update main, rebase the current branch from main, and restore the stash after a clean rebase.
content:
git_rebase_main_safe() {
  branch="$(git branch --show-current)" || return 1
  if [ -z "$branch" ]; then
    echo "Not on a branch."
    return 1
  fi
  if [ "$branch" = "main" ]; then
    echo "Run this from a non-main branch."
    return 1
  fi

  stashed=0
  if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
    git stash push -u -m "safe rebase from main" || return 1
    stashed=1
  fi

  git fetch || return 1
  git switch main || return 1
  if ! git pull --ff-only; then
    git switch "$branch"
    return 1
  fi
  git switch "$branch" || return 1

  if ! git rebase main; then
    echo "Rebase stopped. Resolve conflicts, then run git rebase --continue or git rebase --abort."
    return 1
  fi

  if [ "$stashed" -eq 1 ]; then
    stash_ref='stash@{0}'
    if git stash apply "$stash_ref"; then
      git stash drop "$stash_ref"
    else
      echo "Stash restore stopped. Resolve conflicts before continuing; the stash was kept."
      return 1
    fi
  fi

  git status --short
}

git_rebase_main_safe
